purpose: Define the disabled-by-default runtime file-drop path for approved Momentum source-owner bundles before any canonical source consumption is enabled.
promotionRequirements: Source owners provide approved_source file bundles using the accepted template shape.
Validation confirms brand IDs, source owners, dates, required file kinds, SMD weights, room-to-grow inputs, and movement/significance evidence.
Canonical-use governance approves runtime consumption for the source directory.
Persistence readiness clears canonical source promotion, identity/access, retention/privacy, and backup/recovery blockers.
The runtime keeps source caveats, red momentum visibility, no-causality guardrails, and source-period compatibility attached to every read.
disabledReasons: No approved source-owner file drops are loaded in the repository.
Canonical runtime consumption of source-owner files has not been approved.
Enterprise source promotion governance is still blocked by persistence readiness requirements.
guardrails: Do not auto-consume source-owner files as packet facts until canonical-use governance is approved.
Do not treat browser-local promoted packets or reviewed-local records as canonical source truth.
Do not present approved_source file examples as live source data unless they are loaded from the governed source directory and pass validation.
Keep source-owner files separate from React components and dynamic views; services load and validate, components render.
caveats: This policy defines the future runtime landing zone; it does not prove that approved source files exist.
The current source-owner file-bundle importer supports review and preview, not default runtime source replacement.
